impl_hash_borrow_with_str_and_bytes.description: This lint is concerned with the semantics of `Borrow` and `Hash` for a type that implements all three of `Hash`, `Borrow ` and `Borrow ` as it is impossible to satisfy the semantics of Borrow and `Hash` for both `Borrow ` and `Borrow `.

impl_trait_in_params.title: Impl Trait In Params
impl_trait_in_params.description: Lints when `impl Trait` is being used in a function's parameters.

implicit_clone.title: Implicit Clone
implicit_clone.description: Checks for the usage of `_.to_owned()`, `vec.to_vec()`, or similar when calling `_.clone()` would be clearer.

implicit_hasher.title: Implicit Hasher
implicit_hasher.description: Checks for public `impl` or `fn` missing generalization over different hashers and implicitly defaulting to the default hashing algorithm (`SipHash`).

implicit_return.title: Implicit Return
implicit_return.description: Checks for missing return statements at the end of a block.

implied_bounds_in_impls.title: Implied Bounds In Impls
implied_bounds_in_impls.description: Looks for bounds in `impl Trait` in return position that are implied by other bounds. This can happen when a trait is specified that another trait already has as a supertrait (e.g. `fn() -> impl Deref + DerefMut ` has an unnecessary `Deref` bound, because `Deref` is a supertrait of `DerefMut`)

impossible_comparisons.title: Impossible Comparisons
impossible_comparisons.description: Checks for double comparisons that can never succeed

imprecise_flops.title: Imprecise Flops
imprecise_flops.description: Looks for floating-point expressions that can be expressed using built-in methods to improve accuracy at the cost of performance.

incompatible_msrv.title: Incompatible Msrv
incompatible_msrv.description: This lint checks that no function newer than the defined MSRV (minimum supported rust version) is used in the crate.

inconsistent_digit_grouping.title: Inconsistent Digit Grouping
inconsistent_digit_grouping.description: Warns if an integral or floating-point constant is grouped inconsistently with underscores.

inconsistent_struct_constructor.title: Inconsistent Struct Constructor
inconsistent_struct_constructor.description: Checks for struct constructors where the order of the field init in the constructor is inconsistent with the order in the struct definition.

index_refutable_slice.title: Index Refutable Slice
index_refutable_slice.description: The lint checks for slice bindings in patterns that are only used to access individual slice values.

indexing_slicing.title: Indexing Slicing
indexing_slicing.description: Checks for usage of indexing or slicing that may panic at runtime.

ineffective_bit_mask.title: Ineffective Bit Mask
ineffective_bit_mask.description: Checks for bit masks in comparisons which can be removed without changing the outcome. The basic structure can be seen in the following table:

ineffective_open_options.title: Ineffective Open Options
ineffective_open_options.description: Checks if both `.write(true)` and `.append(true)` methods are called on a same `OpenOptions`.

inefficient_to_string.title: Inefficient To String
inefficient_to_string.description: Checks for usage of `.to_string()` on an `&&T` where `T` implements `ToString` directly (like `&&str` or `&&String`).

infallible_destructuring_match.title: Infallible Destructuring Match
infallible_destructuring_match.description: Checks for matches being used to destructure a single-variant enum or tuple struct where a `let` will suffice.

infallible_try_from.title: Infallible Try From
infallible_try_from.description: Finds manual impls of `TryFrom` with infallible error types.

infinite_iter.title: Infinite Iter
infinite_iter.description: Checks for iteration that is guaranteed to be infinite.

infinite_loop.title: Infinite Loop
infinite_loop.description: Checks for infinite loops in a function where the return type is not `!` and lint accordingly.

inherent_to_string.title: Inherent To String
inherent_to_string.description: Checks for the definition of inherent methods with a signature of `to_string(&self) -> String`.

inherent_to_string_shadow_display.title: Inherent To String Shadow Display
inherent_to_string_shadow_display.description: Checks for the definition of inherent methods with a signature of `to_string(&self) -> String` and if the type implementing this method also implements the `Display` trait.

init_numbered_fields.title: Init Numbered Fields
init_numbered_fields.description: Checks for tuple structs initialized with field syntax. It will however not lint if a base initializer is present. The lint will also ignore code in macros.

inline_always.title: Inline Always
inline_always.description: Checks for items annotated with `#[inline(always)]`, unless the annotated function is empty or simply panics.

inline_asm_x86_att_syntax.title: Inline Asm X86 Att Syntax
inline_asm_x86_att_syntax.description: Checks for usage of AT&T x86 assembly syntax.

inline_asm_x86_intel_syntax.title: Inline Asm X86 Intel Syntax
inline_asm_x86_intel_syntax.description: Checks for usage of Intel x86 assembly syntax.

inline_fn_without_body.title: Inline Fn Without Body
inline_fn_without_body.description: Checks for `#[inline]` on trait methods without bodies

int_plus_one.title: Int Plus One
int_plus_one.description: Checks for usage of `x >= y + 1` or `x - 1 >= y` (and `

integer_division_remainder_used.title: Integer Division Remainder Used
integer_division_remainder_used.description: Checks for the usage of division (`/`) and remainder (`%`) operations when performed on any integer types using the default `Div` and `Rem` trait implementations.

into_iter_on_ref.title: Into Iter On Ref
into_iter_on_ref.description: Checks for `into_iter` calls on references which should be replaced by `iter` or `iter_mut`.

into_iter_without_iter.title: Into Iter Without Iter
into_iter_without_iter.description: This is the opposite of the `iter_without_into_iter` lint. It looks for `IntoIterator for (&|&mut) Type` implementations without an inherent `iter` or `iter_mut` method on the type or on any of the types in its `Deref` chain.

invalid_regex.title: Invalid Regex
invalid_regex.description: Checks regex creation (with `Regex::new`, `RegexBuilder::new`, or `RegexSet::new`) for correct regex syntax.

invalid_upcast_comparisons.title: Invalid Upcast Comparisons
invalid_upcast_comparisons.description: Checks for comparisons where the relation is always either true or false, but where one side has been upcast so that the comparison is necessary. Only integer types are checked.

inverted_saturating_sub.title: Inverted Saturating Sub
inverted_saturating_sub.description: Checks for comparisons between integers, followed by subtracting the greater value from the lower one.

invisible_characters.title: Invisible Characters
invisible_characters.description: Checks for invisible Unicode characters in the code.

io_other_error.title: Io Other Error
io_other_error.description: This lint warns on calling `io::Error::new(..)` with a kind of `io::ErrorKind::Other`.

ip_constant.title: Ip Constant
ip_constant.description: Checks for IP addresses that could be replaced with predefined constants such as `Ipv4Addr::new(127, 0, 0, 1)` instead of using the appropriate constants.

is_digit_ascii_radix.title: Is Digit Ascii Radix
is_digit_ascii_radix.description: Finds usages of `char::is_digit` that can be replaced with `is_ascii_digit` or `is_ascii_hexdigit`.

items_after_statements.title: Items After Statements
items_after_statements.description: Checks for items declared after some statement in a block.

items_after_test_module.title: Items After Test Module
items_after_test_module.description: Triggers if an item is declared after the testing module marked with `#[cfg(test)]`.

iter_cloned_collect.title: Iter Cloned Collect
iter_cloned_collect.description: Checks for the use of `.cloned().collect()` on slice to create a `Vec`.

iter_filter_is_ok.title: Iter Filter Is Ok
iter_filter_is_ok.description: Checks for usage of `.filter(Result::is_ok)` that may be replaced with a `.flatten()` call. This lint will require additional changes to the follow-up calls as it affects the type.

iter_filter_is_some.title: Iter Filter Is Some
iter_filter_is_some.description: Checks for usage of `.filter(Option::is_some)` that may be replaced with a `.flatten()` call. This lint will require additional changes to the follow-up calls as it affects the type.

iter_kv_map.title: Iter Kv Map
iter_kv_map.description: Checks for iterating a map (`HashMap` or `BTreeMap`) and ignoring either the keys or values.

iter_next_slice.title: Iter Next Slice
iter_next_slice.description: Checks for usage of `iter().next()` on a Slice or an Array

iter_not_returning_iterator.title: Iter Not Returning Iterator
iter_not_returning_iterator.description: Detects methods named `iter` or `iter_mut` that do not have a return type that implements `Iterator`.

iter_nth.title: Iter Nth
iter_nth.description: Checks for usage of `.iter().nth()`/`.iter_mut().nth()` on standard library types that have equivalent `.get()`/`.get_mut()` methods.

iter_on_empty_collections.title: Iter On Empty Collections
iter_on_empty_collections.description: Checks for calls to `iter`, `iter_mut` or `into_iter` on empty collections

iter_on_single_items.title: Iter On Single Items
iter_on_single_items.description: Checks for calls to `iter`, `iter_mut` or `into_iter` on collections containing a single item

iter_out_of_bounds.title: Iter Out Of Bounds
iter_out_of_bounds.description: Looks for iterator combinator calls such as `.take(x)` or `.skip(x)` where `x` is greater than the amount of items that an iterator will produce.

iter_over_hash_type.title: Iter Over Hash Type
iter_over_hash_type.description: This is a restriction lint which prevents the use of hash types (i.e., `HashSet` and `HashMap`) in for loops.

iter_overeager_cloned.title: Iter Overeager Cloned
iter_overeager_cloned.description: Checks for usage of `_.cloned(). ()` where call to `.cloned()` can be postponed.

iter_skip_next.title: Iter Skip Next
iter_skip_next.description: Checks for usage of `.skip(x).next()` on iterators.

iter_skip_zero.title: Iter Skip Zero
iter_skip_zero.description: Checks for usage of `.skip(0)` on iterators.

iter_with_drain.title: Iter With Drain
iter_with_drain.description: Checks for usage of `.drain(..)` on `Vec` and `VecDeque` for iteration.

iter_without_into_iter.title: Iter Without Into Iter
iter_without_into_iter.description: Looks for `iter` and `iter_mut` methods without an associated `IntoIterator for (&|&mut) Type` implementation.

iterator_step_by_zero.title: Iterator Step By Zero
iterator_step_by_zero.description: Checks for calling `.step_by(0)` on iterators which panics.

join_absolute_paths.title: Join Absolute Paths
join_absolute_paths.description: Checks for calls to `Path::join` that start with a path separator (`\\` or `/`).

just_underscores_and_digits.title: Just Underscores And Digits
just_underscores_and_digits.description: Checks if you have variables whose name consists of just underscores and digits.

large_const_arrays.title: Large Const Arrays
large_const_arrays.description: Checks for large `const` arrays that should be defined as `static` instead.

large_digit_groups.title: Large Digit Groups
large_digit_groups.description: Warns if the digits of an integral or floating-point constant are grouped into groups that are too large.

large_enum_variant.title: Large Enum Variant
large_enum_variant.description: Checks for large size differences between variants on `enum`s.

large_futures.title: Large Futures
large_futures.description: It checks for the size of a `Future` created by `async fn` or `async {}`.

large_include_file.title: Large Include File
large_include_file.description: Checks for the inclusion of large files via `include_bytes!()` or `include_str!()`.

large_stack_arrays.title: Large Stack Arrays
large_stack_arrays.description: Checks for local arrays that may be too large.

large_stack_frames.title: Large Stack Frames
large_stack_frames.description: Checks for functions that use a lot of stack space.

large_types_passed_by_value.title: Large Types Passed By Value
large_types_passed_by_value.description: Checks for functions taking arguments by value, where the argument type is `Copy` and large enough to be worth considering passing by reference. Does not trigger if the function is being exported, because that might induce API breakage, if the parameter is declared as mutable, or if the argument is a `self`.

len_without_is_empty.title: Len Without Is Empty
len_without_is_empty.description: Checks for items that implement `.len()` but not `.is_empty()`.

len_zero.title: Len Zero
len_zero.description: Checks for getting the length of something via `.len()` just to compare to zero, and suggests using `.is_empty()` where applicable.

let_and_return.title: Let And Return
let_and_return.description: Checks for `let`-bindings, which are subsequently returned.

let_underscore_future.title: Let Underscore Future
let_underscore_future.description: Checks for `let _ = ` where the resulting type of expr implements `Future`

let_underscore_lock.title: Let Underscore Lock
let_underscore_lock.description: Checks for `let _ = sync_lock`. This supports `mutex` and `rwlock` in `parking_lot`. For `std` locks see the `rustc` lint `let_underscore_lock`

let_underscore_untyped.title: Let Underscore Untyped
let_underscore_untyped.description: Checks for `let _ = ` without a type annotation, and suggests to either provide one, or remove the `let` keyword altogether.

let_unit_value.title: Let Unit Value
let_unit_value.description: Checks for binding a unit value.

let_with_type_underscore.title: Let With Type Underscore
let_with_type_underscore.description: Detects when a variable is declared with an explicit type of `_`.

lines_filter_map_ok.title: Lines Filter Map Ok
lines_filter_map_ok.description: Checks for usage of `lines.filter_map(Result::ok)` or `lines.flat_map(Result::ok)` when `lines` has type `std::io::Lines`.

linkedlist.title: Linkedlist
linkedlist.description: Checks for usage of any `LinkedList`, suggesting to use a `Vec` or a `VecDeque` (formerly called `RingBuf`).

lint_groups_priority.title: Lint Groups Priority
lint_groups_priority.description: Checks for lint groups with the same priority as lints in the `Cargo.toml` `[lints]` table.

literal_string_with_formatting_args.title: Literal String With Formatting Args
literal_string_with_formatting_args.description: Checks if string literals have formatting arguments outside of macros using them (like `format!`).

little_endian_bytes.title: Little Endian Bytes
little_endian_bytes.description: Checks for the usage of the `to_le_bytes` method and/or the function `from_le_bytes`.

lossy_float_literal.title: Lossy Float Literal
lossy_float_literal.description: Checks for whole number float literals that cannot be represented as the underlying type without loss.

macro_metavars_in_unsafe.title: Macro Metavars In Unsafe
macro_metavars_in_unsafe.description: Looks for macros that expand metavariables in an unsafe block.

main_recursion.title: Main Recursion
main_recursion.description: Checks for recursion using the entrypoint.

manual_abs_diff.title: Manual Abs Diff
manual_abs_diff.description: Detects patterns like `if a > b { a - b } else { b - a }` and suggests using `a.abs_diff(b)`.

manual_assert.title: Manual Assert
manual_assert.description: Detects `if`-then-`panic!` that can be replaced with `assert!`.

manual_async_fn.title: Manual Async Fn
manual_async_fn.description: It checks for manual implementations of `async` functions.

manual_bits.title: Manual Bits
manual_bits.description: Checks for usage of `size_of:: () * 8` when `T::BITS` is available.

manual_c_str_literals.title: Manual C Str Literals
manual_c_str_literals.description: Checks for the manual creation of C strings (a string with a `NUL` byte at the end), either through one of the `CStr` constructor functions, or more plainly by calling `.as_ptr()` on a (byte) string literal with a hardcoded `\0` byte at the end.

manual_checked_ops.title: Manual Checked Ops
manual_checked_ops.description: Detects manual zero checks before dividing integers, such as `if x!= 0 { y / x }`.

manual_clamp.title: Manual Clamp
manual_clamp.description: Identifies good opportunities for a clamp function from std or core, and suggests using it.

manual_contains.title: Manual Contains
manual_contains.description: Checks for usage of `iter().any()` on slices when it can be replaced with `contains()` and suggests doing so.

manual_dangling_ptr.title: Manual Dangling Ptr
manual_dangling_ptr.description: Checks for casts of small constant literals or `mem::align_of` results to raw pointers.

manual_div_ceil.title: Manual Div Ceil
manual_div_ceil.description: Checks for an expression like `(x + (y - 1)) / y` which is a common manual reimplementation of `x.div_ceil(y)`.

manual_filter.title: Manual Filter
manual_filter.description: Checks for usage of `match` which could be implemented using `filter`

manual_filter_map.title: Manual Filter Map
manual_filter_map.description: Checks for usage of `_.filter(_).map(_)` that can be written more simply as `filter_map(_)`.

manual_find.title: Manual Find
manual_find.description: Checks for manual implementations of Iterator::find

manual_find_map.title: Manual Find Map
manual_find_map.description: Checks for usage of `_.find(_).map(_)` that can be written more simply as `find_map(_)`.

manual_flatten.title: Manual Flatten
manual_flatten.description: Checks for unnecessary `if let` usage in a for loop where only the `Some` or `Ok` variant of the iterator element is used.

manual_hash_one.title: Manual Hash One
manual_hash_one.description: Checks for cases where `BuildHasher::hash_one` can be used.

manual_ignore_case_cmp.title: Manual Ignore Case Cmp
manual_ignore_case_cmp.description: Checks for manual case-insensitive ASCII comparison.

manual_ilog2.title: Manual Ilog2
manual_ilog2.description: Checks for expressions like `N - x.leading_zeros()` (where `N` is one less than bit width of `x`) or `x.ilog(2)`, which are manual reimplementations of `x.ilog2()`

manual_inspect.title: Manual Inspect
manual_inspect.description: Checks for uses of `map` which return the original item.

manual_is_ascii_check.title: Manual Is Ascii Check
manual_is_ascii_check.description: Suggests to use dedicated built-in methods, `is_ascii_(lowercase|uppercase|digit|hexdigit)` for checking on corresponding ascii range

manual_is_multiple_of.title: Manual Is Multiple Of
manual_is_multiple_of.description: Checks for manual implementation of `.is_multiple_of()` on unsigned integer types.

manual_is_power_of_two.title: Manual Is Power Of Two
manual_is_power_of_two.description: Checks for expressions like `x.count_ones() == 1` or `x & (x - 1) == 0`, with x and unsigned integer, which may be manual reimplementations of `x.is_power_of_two()`.

manual_is_variant_and.title: Manual Is Variant And
manual_is_variant_and.description: Checks for usage of `option.map(f).unwrap_or_default()` and `result.map(f).unwrap_or_default()` where `f` is a function or closure that returns the `bool` type.

manual_let_else.title: Manual Let Else
manual_let_else.description: Warn of cases where `let...else` could be used

manual_main_separator_str.title: Manual Main Separator Str
manual_main_separator_str.description: Checks for references on `std::path::MAIN_SEPARATOR.to_string()` used to build a `&str`.

manual_map.title: Manual Map
manual_map.description: Checks for usage of `match` which could be implemented using `map`

manual_memcpy.title: Manual Memcpy
manual_memcpy.description: Checks for for-loops that manually copy items between slices that could be optimized by having a memcpy.

manual_non_exhaustive.title: Manual Non Exhaustive
manual_non_exhaustive.description: Checks for manual implementations of the non-exhaustive pattern.

manual_noop_waker.title: Manual Noop Waker
manual_noop_waker.description: Checks for manual implementations of `std::task::Wake` that are empty.

manual_ok_err.title: Manual Ok Err
manual_ok_err.description: Checks for manual implementation of `.ok()` or `.err()` on `Result` values.

manual_option_as_slice.title: Manual Option As Slice
manual_option_as_slice.description: This detects various manual reimplementations of `Option::as_slice`.

manual_option_zip.title: Manual Option Zip
manual_option_zip.description: Checks for usage of `a.and_then(|a| b.map(|b| (a, b)))` which can be more concisely expressed as `a.zip(b)`.

manual_pattern_char_comparison.title: Manual Pattern Char Comparison
manual_pattern_char_comparison.description: Checks for manual `char` comparison in string patterns

manual_pop_if.title: Manual Pop If
manual_pop_if.description: Checks for code to be replaced by `pop_if` methods.

manual_range_patterns.title: Manual Range Patterns
manual_range_patterns.description: Looks for combined OR patterns that are all contained in a specific range, e.g. `6 | 4 | 5 | 9 | 7 | 8` can be rewritten as `4..=9`.

manual_rem_euclid.title: Manual Rem Euclid
manual_rem_euclid.description: Checks for an expression like `((x % 4) + 4) % 4` which is a common manual reimplementation of `x.rem_euclid(4)`.

manual_repeat_n.title: Manual Repeat N
manual_repeat_n.description: Checks for `repeat().take()` that can be replaced with `repeat_n()`.

manual_retain.title: Manual Retain
manual_retain.description: Checks for code to be replaced by `.retain()`.

manual_rotate.title: Manual Rotate
manual_rotate.description: It detects manual bit rotations that could be rewritten using standard functions `rotate_left` or `rotate_right`.

manual_slice_fill.title: Manual Slice Fill
manual_slice_fill.description: Checks for manually filling a slice with a value.

manual_slice_size_calculation.title: Manual Slice Size Calculation
manual_slice_size_calculation.description: When `a` is `&[T]`, detect `a.len() * size_of:: ()` and suggest `size_of_val(a)` instead.

manual_string_new.title: Manual String New
manual_string_new.description: Checks for usage of `""` to create a `String`, such as `"".to_string()`, `"".to_owned()`, `String::from("")` and others.

manual_strip.title: Manual Strip
manual_strip.description: Suggests using `strip_{prefix,suffix}` over `str::{starts,ends}_with` and slicing using the pattern's length.

manual_try_fold.title: Manual Try Fold
manual_try_fold.description: Checks for usage of `Iterator::fold` with a type that implements `Try`.

manual_unwrap_or_default.title: Manual Unwrap Or Default
manual_unwrap_or_default.description: Checks if a `match` or `if let` expression can be simplified using `.unwrap_or_default()`.

manual_while_let_some.title: Manual While Let Some
manual_while_let_some.description: Looks for loops that check for emptiness of a `Vec` in the condition and pop an element in the body as a separate operation.

many_single_char_names.title: Many Single Char Names
many_single_char_names.description: Checks for too many variables whose name consists of a single character.

map_all_any_identity.title: Map All Any Identity
map_all_any_identity.description: Checks for usage of `.map(…)`, followed by `.all(identity)` or `.any(identity)`.

map_clone.title: Map Clone
map_clone.description: Checks for usage of `map(|x| x.clone())` or dereferencing closures for `Copy` types, on `Iterator` or `Option`, and suggests `cloned()` or `copied()` instead

map_entry.title: Map Entry
map_entry.description: Checks for usage of `contains_key` + `insert` on `HashMap` or `BTreeMap`.

map_flatten.title: Map Flatten
map_flatten.description: Checks for usage of `_.map(_).flatten(_)` on `Iterator` and `Option`

map_identity.title: Map Identity
map_identity.description: Checks for instances of `map(f)` where `f` is the identity function.

map_unwrap_or.title: Map Unwrap Or
map_unwrap_or.description: Checks for usage of `option.map(_).unwrap_or(_)` or `option.map(_).unwrap_or_else(_)` or `result.map(_).unwrap_or_else(_)`.

map_with_unused_argument_over_ranges.title: Map With Unused Argument Over Ranges
map_with_unused_argument_over_ranges.description: Checks for `Iterator::map` over ranges without using the parameter which could be more clearly expressed using `std::iter::repeat(...).take(...)` or `std::iter::repeat_n`.

match_as_ref.title: Match As Ref
match_as_ref.description: Checks for match which is used to add a reference to an `Option` value.

match_bool.title: Match Bool
match_bool.description: Checks for matches where match expression is a `bool`. It suggests to replace the expression with an `if...else` block.

match_like_matches_macro.title: Match Like Matches Macro
match_like_matches_macro.description: Checks for `match` or `if let` expressions producing a `bool` that could be written using `matches!`

match_on_vec_items.title: Match On Vec Items
match_on_vec_items.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

match_ref_pats.title: Match Ref Pats
match_ref_pats.description: Checks for matches where all arms match a reference, suggesting to remove the reference and deref the matched expression instead. It also checks for `if let &foo = bar` blocks.

match_same_arms.title: Match Same Arms
match_same_arms.description: Checks for `match` with identical arm bodies.

match_single_binding.title: Match Single Binding
match_single_binding.description: Checks for useless match that binds to only one value.

match_str_case_mismatch.title: Match Str Case Mismatch
match_str_case_mismatch.description: Checks for `match` expressions modifying the case of a string with non-compliant arms

match_wild_err_arm.title: Match Wild Err Arm
match_wild_err_arm.description: Checks for arm which matches all errors with `Err(_)` and take drastic actions like `panic!`.

match_wildcard_for_single_variants.title: Match Wildcard For Single Variants
match_wildcard_for_single_variants.description: Checks for wildcard enum matches for a single variant.

maybe_infinite_iter.title: Maybe Infinite Iter
maybe_infinite_iter.description: Checks for iteration that may be infinite.

mem_forget.title: Mem Forget
mem_forget.description: Checks for usage of `std::mem::forget(t)` where `t` is `Drop` or has a field that implements `Drop`.

mem_replace_with_default.title: Mem Replace With Default
mem_replace_with_default.description: Checks for `std::mem::replace` on a value of type `T` with `T::default()`.

min_ident_chars.title: Min Ident Chars
min_ident_chars.description: Checks for identifiers which consist of a single character (or fewer than the configured threshold).

min_max.title: Min Max
min_max.description: Checks for expressions where `std::cmp::min` and `max` are used to clamp values, but switched so that the result is constant.

misaligned_transmute.title: Misaligned Transmute
misaligned_transmute.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

mismatching_type_param_order.title: Mismatching Type Param Order
mismatching_type_param_order.description: Checks for type parameters which are positioned inconsistently between a type definition and impl block. Specifically, a parameter in an impl block which has the same name as a parameter in the type def, but is in a different place.

misnamed_getters.title: Misnamed Getters
misnamed_getters.description: Checks for getter methods that return a field that doesn't correspond to the name of the method, when there is a field's whose name matches that of the method.

misrefactored_assign_op.title: Misrefactored Assign Op
misrefactored_assign_op.description: Checks for `a op= a op b` or `a op= b op a` patterns.

missing_assert_message.title: Missing Assert Message
missing_assert_message.description: Checks assertions without a custom panic message.

missing_asserts_for_indexing.title: Missing Asserts For Indexing
missing_asserts_for_indexing.description: Checks for repeated slice indexing without asserting beforehand that the length is greater than the largest index used to index into the slice.

missing_const_for_fn.title: Missing Const For Fn
missing_const_for_fn.description: Suggests the use of `const` in functions and methods where possible.

missing_const_for_thread_local.title: Missing Const For Thread Local
missing_const_for_thread_local.description: Suggests to use `const` in `thread_local!` macro if possible.

missing_docs_in_private_items.title: Missing Docs In Private Items
missing_docs_in_private_items.description: Warns if there is missing documentation for any private documentable item.

missing_enforced_import_renames.title: Missing Enforced Import Renames
missing_enforced_import_renames.description: Checks for imports that do not rename the item as specified in the `enforced-import-renames` config option.

missing_errors_doc.title: Missing Errors Doc
missing_errors_doc.description: Checks the doc comments of publicly visible functions that return a `Result` type and warns if there is no `# Errors` section.

missing_fields_in_debug.title: Missing Fields In Debug
missing_fields_in_debug.description: Checks for manual `core::fmt::Debug` implementations that do not use all fields.

missing_inline_in_public_items.title: Missing Inline In Public Items
missing_inline_in_public_items.description: It lints if an exported function, method, trait method with default impl, or trait method impl is not `#[inline]`.

missing_panics_doc.title: Missing Panics Doc
missing_panics_doc.description: Checks the doc comments of publicly visible functions that may panic and warns if there is no `# Panics` section.

missing_safety_doc.title: Missing Safety Doc
missing_safety_doc.description: Checks for the doc comments of publicly visible unsafe functions and warns if there is no `# Safety` section.

missing_trait_methods.title: Missing Trait Methods
missing_trait_methods.description: Checks if a provided method is used implicitly by a trait implementation.

missing_transmute_annotations.title: Missing Transmute Annotations
missing_transmute_annotations.description: Checks if transmute calls have all generics specified.

mistyped_literal_suffixes.title: Mistyped Literal Suffixes
mistyped_literal_suffixes.description: Warns for mistyped suffix in literals

mixed_attributes_style.title: Mixed Attributes Style
mixed_attributes_style.description: Checks for items that have the same kind of attributes with mixed styles (inner/outer).

mixed_case_hex_literals.title: Mixed Case Hex Literals
mixed_case_hex_literals.description: Warns on hexadecimal literals with mixed-case letter digits.

mixed_read_write_in_expression.title: Mixed Read Write In Expression
mixed_read_write_in_expression.description: Checks for a read and a write to the same variable where whether the read occurs before or after the write depends on the evaluation order of sub-expressions.

mod_module_files.title: Mod Module Files
mod_module_files.description: Checks that module layout uses only self named module files; bans `mod.rs` files.

module_inception.title: Module Inception
module_inception.description: Checks for modules that have the same name as their parent module

module_name_repetitions.title: Module Name Repetitions
module_name_repetitions.description: Detects public item names that are prefixed or suffixed by the containing public module's name.

modulo_one.title: Modulo One
modulo_one.description: Checks for getting the remainder of integer division by one or minus one.

multiple_bound_locations.title: Multiple Bound Locations
multiple_bound_locations.description: Check if a generic is defined both in the bound predicate and in the `where` clause.

multiple_crate_versions.title: Multiple Crate Versions
multiple_crate_versions.description: Checks to see if multiple versions of a crate are being used.

multiple_inherent_impl.title: Multiple Inherent Impl
multiple_inherent_impl.description: Checks for multiple inherent implementations of a struct

multiple_unsafe_ops_per_block.title: Multiple Unsafe Ops Per Block
multiple_unsafe_ops_per_block.description: Checks for `unsafe` blocks that contain more than one unsafe operation.

must_use_candidate.title: Must Use Candidate
must_use_candidate.description: Checks for public functions that have no `#[must_use]` attribute, but return something not already marked must-use, have no mutable arg and mutate no statics.

must_use_unit.title: Must Use Unit
must_use_unit.description: Checks for a `#[must_use]` attribute on unit-returning functions and methods.

mut_from_ref.title: Mut From Ref
mut_from_ref.description: This lint checks for functions that take immutable references and return mutable ones. This will not trigger if no unsafe code exists as there are multiple safe functions which will do this transformation

mut_mut.title: Mut Mut
mut_mut.description: Checks for instances of `mut mut` references.

mut_range_bound.title: Mut Range Bound
mut_range_bound.description: Checks for loops with a range bound that is a mutable variable.

mutable_key_type.title: Mutable Key Type
mutable_key_type.description: Checks for sets/maps with mutable key types.

mutex_atomic.title: Mutex Atomic
mutex_atomic.description: Checks for usage of `Mutex ` where an atomic will do.

mutex_integer.title: Mutex Integer
mutex_integer.description: Checks for usage of `Mutex ` where `X` is an integral type.

needless_arbitrary_self_type.title: Needless Arbitrary Self Type
needless_arbitrary_self_type.description: The lint checks for `self` in fn parameters that specify the `Self`-type explicitly

needless_as_bytes.title: Needless As Bytes
needless_as_bytes.description: It detects useless calls to `str::as_bytes()` before calling `len()` or `is_empty()`.

needless_bitwise_bool.title: Needless Bitwise Bool
needless_bitwise_bool.description: Checks for usage of bitwise and/or operators between booleans, where performance may be improved by using a lazy and.

needless_bool.title: Needless Bool
needless_bool.description: Checks for expressions of the form `if c { true } else { false }` (or vice versa) and suggests using the condition directly.

needless_bool_assign.title: Needless Bool Assign
needless_bool_assign.description: Checks for expressions of the form `if c { x = true } else { x = false }` (or vice versa) and suggest assigning the variable directly from the condition.

needless_borrow.title: Needless Borrow
needless_borrow.description: Checks for address of operations (`&`) that are going to be dereferenced immediately by the compiler.

needless_borrowed_reference.title: Needless Borrowed Reference
needless_borrowed_reference.description: Checks for bindings that needlessly destructure a reference and borrow the inner value with `&ref`.

needless_borrows_for_generic_args.title: Needless Borrows For Generic Args
needless_borrows_for_generic_args.description: Checks for borrow operations (`&`) that are used as a generic argument to a function when the borrowed value could be used.

needless_character_iteration.title: Needless Character Iteration
needless_character_iteration.description: Checks if an iterator is used to check if a string is ascii.

needless_collect.title: Needless Collect
needless_collect.description: Checks for functions collecting an iterator when collect is not needed.

needless_continue.title: Needless Continue
needless_continue.description: The lint checks for `if`-statements appearing in loops that contain a `continue` statement in either their main blocks or their `else`-blocks, when omitting the `else`-block possibly with some rearrangement of code can make the code easier to understand. The lint also checks if the last statement in the loop is a `continue`

needless_for_each.title: Needless For Each
needless_for_each.description: Checks for usage of `for_each` that would be more simply written as a `for` loop.

needless_ifs.title: Needless Ifs
needless_ifs.description: Checks for empty `if` branches with no else branch.

needless_late_init.title: Needless Late Init
needless_late_init.description: Checks for late initializations that can be replaced by a `let` statement with an initializer.

needless_lifetimes.title: Needless Lifetimes
needless_lifetimes.description: Checks for lifetime annotations which can be removed by relying on lifetime elision.

needless_match.title: Needless Match
needless_match.description: Checks for unnecessary `match` or match-like `if let` returns for `Option` and `Result` when function signatures are the same.

needless_maybe_sized.title: Needless Maybe Sized
needless_maybe_sized.description: Lints `?Sized` bounds applied to type parameters that cannot be unsized

needless_option_as_deref.title: Needless Option As Deref
needless_option_as_deref.description: Checks for no-op uses of `Option::{as_deref, as_deref_mut}`, for example, `Option::as_deref()` returns the same type.

needless_parens_on_range_literals.title: Needless Parens On Range Literals
needless_parens_on_range_literals.description: The lint checks for parenthesis on literals in range statements that are superfluous.

needless_pass_by_ref_mut.title: Needless Pass By Ref Mut
needless_pass_by_ref_mut.description: Check if a `&mut` function argument is actually used mutably.

needless_pass_by_value.title: Needless Pass By Value
needless_pass_by_value.description: Checks for functions taking arguments by value, but not consuming them in its body.

needless_pub_self.title: Needless Pub Self
needless_pub_self.description: Checks for usage of `pub(self)` and `pub(in self)`.

needless_question_mark.title: Needless Question Mark
needless_question_mark.description: Suggests replacing `Ok(x?)` or `Some(x?)` with `x` in return positions where the `?` operator is not needed to convert the type of `x`.

needless_range_loop.title: Needless Range Loop
needless_range_loop.description: Checks for looping over the range of `0..len` of some collection just to get the values by index.

needless_raw_string_hashes.title: Needless Raw String Hashes
needless_raw_string_hashes.description: Checks for raw string literals with an unnecessary amount of hashes around them.

needless_raw_strings.title: Needless Raw Strings
needless_raw_strings.description: Checks for raw string literals where a string literal can be used instead.

needless_return.title: Needless Return
needless_return.description: Checks for return statements at the end of a block.

needless_return_with_question_mark.title: Needless Return With Question Mark
needless_return_with_question_mark.description: Checks for return statements on `Err` paired with the `?` operator.

needless_splitn.title: Needless Splitn
needless_splitn.description: Checks for usage of `str::splitn` (or `str::rsplitn`) where using `str::split` would be the same.

needless_type_cast.title: Needless Type Cast
needless_type_cast.description: Checks for bindings (constants, statics, or let bindings) that are defined with one numeric type but are consistently cast to a different type in all usages.

needless_update.title: Needless Update
needless_update.description: Checks for needlessly including a base struct on update when all fields are changed anyway.

neg_cmp_op_on_partial_ord.title: Neg Cmp Op On Partial Ord
neg_cmp_op_on_partial_ord.description: Checks for the usage of negated comparison operators on types which only implement `PartialOrd` (e.g., `f64`).

neg_multiply.title: Neg Multiply
neg_multiply.description: Checks for multiplication by -1 as a form of negation.

negative_feature_names.title: Negative Feature Names
negative_feature_names.description: Checks for negative feature names with prefix `no-` or `not-`

never_loop.title: Never Loop
never_loop.description: Checks for loops that will always `break`, `return` or `continue` an outer loop.

new_ret_no_self.title: New Ret No Self
new_ret_no_self.description: Checks for `new` not returning a type that contains `Self`.

new_without_default.title: New Without Default
new_without_default.description: Checks for public types with a `pub fn new() -> Self` method and no implementation of `Default`.

no_effect.title: No Effect
no_effect.description: Checks for statements which have no effect.

no_effect_replace.title: No Effect Replace
no_effect_replace.description: Checks for `replace` statements which have no effect.

no_effect_underscore_binding.title: No Effect Underscore Binding
no_effect_underscore_binding.description: Checks for binding to underscore prefixed variable without side-effects.

no_mangle_with_rust_abi.title: No Mangle With Rust Abi
no_mangle_with_rust_abi.description: Checks for Rust ABI functions with the `#[no_mangle]` attribute.

non_ascii_literal.title: Non Ascii Literal
non_ascii_literal.description: Checks for non-ASCII characters in string and char literals.

non_canonical_clone_impl.title: Non Canonical Clone Impl
non_canonical_clone_impl.description: Checks for non-canonical implementations of `Clone` when `Copy` is already implemented.

non_canonical_partial_ord_impl.title: Non Canonical Partial Ord Impl
non_canonical_partial_ord_impl.description: Checks for non-canonical implementations of `PartialOrd` when `Ord` is already implemented.

non_minimal_cfg.title: Non Minimal Cfg
non_minimal_cfg.description: Checks for `any` and `all` combinators in `cfg` with only one condition.

non_octal_unix_permissions.title: Non Octal Unix Permissions
non_octal_unix_permissions.description: Checks for non-octal values used to set Unix file permissions.

non_send_fields_in_send_ty.title: Non Send Fields In Send Ty
non_send_fields_in_send_ty.description: This lint warns about a `Send` implementation for a type that contains fields that are not safe to be sent across threads. It tries to detect fields that can cause a soundness issue when sent to another thread (e.g., `Rc`) while allowing `!Send` fields that are expected to exist in a `Send` type, such as raw pointers.

non_std_lazy_statics.title: Non Std Lazy Statics
non_std_lazy_statics.description: Lints when `once_cell::sync::Lazy` or `lazy_static!` are used to define a static variable, and suggests replacing such cases with `std::sync::LazyLock` instead.

non_zero_suggestions.title: Non Zero Suggestions
non_zero_suggestions.description: Checks for conversions from `NonZero` types to regular integer types, and suggests using `NonZero` types for the target as well.

nonminimal_bool.title: Nonminimal Bool
nonminimal_bool.description: Checks for boolean expressions that can be written more concisely.

nonsensical_open_options.title: Nonsensical Open Options
nonsensical_open_options.description: Checks for duplicate open options as well as combinations that make no sense.

nonstandard_macro_braces.title: Nonstandard Macro Braces
nonstandard_macro_braces.description: Checks that common macros are used with consistent bracing.

not_unsafe_ptr_arg_deref.title: Not Unsafe Ptr Arg Deref
not_unsafe_ptr_arg_deref.description: Checks for public functions that dereference raw pointer arguments but are not marked `unsafe`.

nursery.title: Nursery
nursery.description: The `clippy::nursery` group contains lints which are buggy or need more work. It is not recommended to enable the whole group, but rather cherry-pick lints that are useful for your code base and your use case.

obfuscated_if_else.title: Obfuscated If Else
obfuscated_if_else.description: Checks for unnecessary method chains that can be simplified into `if.. else..`.

octal_escapes.title: Octal Escapes
octal_escapes.description: Checks for `\0` escapes in string and byte literals that look like octal character escapes in C.

only_used_in_recursion.title: Only Used In Recursion
only_used_in_recursion.description: Checks for arguments that are only used in recursion with no side-effects.

op_ref.title: Op Ref
op_ref.description: Checks for arguments to `==` which have their address taken to satisfy a bound and suggests to dereference the other argument instead

option_as_ref_cloned.title: Option As Ref Cloned
option_as_ref_cloned.description: Checks for usage of `.as_ref().cloned()` and `.as_mut().cloned()` on `Option`s

option_as_ref_deref.title: Option As Ref Deref
option_as_ref_deref.description: Checks for usage of `_.as_ref().map(Deref::deref)` or its aliases (such as String::as_str).

option_env_unwrap.title: Option Env Unwrap
option_env_unwrap.description: Checks for usage of `option_env!(...).unwrap()` and suggests usage of the `env!` macro.

option_filter_map.title: Option Filter Map
option_filter_map.description: Checks for iterators of `Option`s using `.filter(Option::is_some).map(Option::unwrap)` that may be replaced with a `.flatten()` call.

option_if_let_else.title: Option If Let Else
option_if_let_else.description: Lints usage of `if let Some(v) =... { y } else { x }` and `match.. { Some(v) => y, None/_ => x }` which are more idiomatically done with `Option::map_or` (if the else bit is a pure expression) or `Option::map_or_else` (if the else bit is an impure expression).

option_map_or_err_ok.title: Option Map Or Err Ok
option_map_or_err_ok.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

option_map_unit_fn.title: Option Map Unit Fn
option_map_unit_fn.description: Checks for usage of `option.map(f)` where f is a function or closure that returns the unit type `()`.

option_option.title: Option Option
option_option.description: Checks for usage of `Option >` in function signatures and type definitions

or_fun_call.title: Or Fun Call
or_fun_call.description: Checks for calls to `.or(foo(..))`, `.unwrap_or(foo(..))`, `.or_insert(foo(..))` etc., and suggests to use `.or_else(|| foo(..))`, `.unwrap_or_else(|| foo(..))`, `.unwrap_or_default()` or `.or_default()` etc. instead.

or_then_unwrap.title: Or Then Unwrap
or_then_unwrap.description: Checks for `.or(…).unwrap()` calls to Options and Results.

out_of_bounds_indexing.title: Out Of Bounds Indexing
out_of_bounds_indexing.description: Checks for out of bounds array indexing with a constant index.

overly_complex_bool_expr.title: Overly Complex Bool Expr
overly_complex_bool_expr.description: Checks for boolean expressions that contain terminals that can be eliminated.

panic_in_result_fn.title: Panic In Result Fn
panic_in_result_fn.description: Checks for usage of `panic!` or assertions in a function whose return type is `Result`.

panicking_unwrap.title: Panicking Unwrap
panicking_unwrap.description: Checks for calls of `unwrap[_err]()` that will always fail.

partial_pub_fields.title: Partial Pub Fields
partial_pub_fields.description: Checks whether some but not all fields of a `struct` are public.

partialeq_to_none.title: Partialeq To None
partialeq_to_none.description: Checks for binary comparisons to a literal `Option::None`.

path_buf_push_overwrite.title: Path Buf Push Overwrite
path_buf_push_overwrite.description: Checks for push calls on `PathBuf` that can cause overwrites.

path_ends_with_ext.title: Path Ends With Ext
path_ends_with_ext.description: Looks for calls to `Path::ends_with` calls where the argument looks like a file extension.

pathbuf_init_then_push.title: Pathbuf Init Then Push
pathbuf_init_then_push.description: Checks for calls to `push` immediately after creating a new `PathBuf`.

pattern_type_mismatch.title: Pattern Type Mismatch
pattern_type_mismatch.description: Checks for patterns that aren't exact representations of the types they are applied to.

pedantic.title: Pedantic
pedantic.description: The `clippy::pedantic` group makes Clippy even more pedantic. You can enable the whole group with `#![warn(clippy::pedantic)]` in the `lib.rs`/`main.rs` of your crate. This lint group is for Clippy power users that want an in depth check of their code.

perf.title: Perf
perf.description: The `clippy::perf` group gives you suggestions on how you can increase the performance of your code. Those lints are mostly about code that the compiler can't trivially optimize, but has to be written in a slightly different way to make the optimizer job easier.

permissions_set_readonly_false.title: Permissions Set Readonly False
permissions_set_readonly_false.description: Checks for calls to `std::fs::Permissions.set_readonly` with argument `false`.

pointer_format.title: Pointer Format
pointer_format.description: Detects pointer format as well as `Debug` formatting of raw pointers or function pointers or any types that have a derived `Debug` impl that recursively contains them.

pointers_in_nomem_asm_block.title: Pointers In Nomem Asm Block
pointers_in_nomem_asm_block.description: Checks if any pointer is being passed to an asm! block with `nomem` option.

possible_missing_comma.title: Possible Missing Comma
possible_missing_comma.description: Checks for possible missing comma in an array. It lints if an array element is a binary operator expression and it lies on two lines.

possible_missing_else.title: Possible Missing Else
possible_missing_else.description: Checks for an `if` expression followed by either a block or another `if` that looks like it should have an `else` between them.

precedence.title: Precedence
precedence.description: Checks for operations where precedence may be unclear and suggests to add parentheses. It catches a mixed usage of arithmetic and bit shifting/combining operators, as well as method calls applied to closures.

precedence_bits.title: Precedence Bits
precedence_bits.description: Checks for bit shifting operations combined with bit masking/combining operators and suggest using parentheses.

print_in_format_impl.title: Print In Format Impl
print_in_format_impl.description: Checks for usage of `println`, `print`, `eprintln` or `eprint` in an implementation of a formatting trait.

print_literal.title: Print Literal
print_literal.description: This lint warns about the use of literals as `print!`/`println!` args.

print_stderr.title: Print Stderr
print_stderr.description: Checks for printing on stderr. The purpose of this lint is to catch debugging remnants.

print_stdout.title: Print Stdout
print_stdout.description: Checks for printing on stdout. The purpose of this lint is to catch debugging remnants.

print_with_newline.title: Print With Newline
print_with_newline.description: This lint warns when you use `print!()` with a format string that ends in a newline.

println_empty_string.title: Println Empty String
println_empty_string.description: This lint warns when you use `println!("")` to print a newline.

ptr_arg.title: Ptr Arg
ptr_arg.description: This lint checks for function arguments of type `&String`, `&Vec`, `&PathBuf`, and `Cow `. It will also suggest you replace `.clone()` calls with the appropriate `.to_owned()`/`to_string()` calls.

ptr_as_ptr.title: Ptr As Ptr
ptr_as_ptr.description: Checks for `as` casts between raw pointers that don't change their constness, namely `*const T` to `*const U` and `*mut T` to `*mut U`.

ptr_cast_constness.title: Ptr Cast Constness
ptr_cast_constness.description: Checks for `as` casts between raw pointers that change their constness, namely `*const T` to `*mut T` and `*mut T` to `*const T`.

ptr_offset_by_literal.title: Ptr Offset By Literal
ptr_offset_by_literal.description: Checks for usage of the `offset` pointer method with an integer literal.

ptr_offset_with_cast.title: Ptr Offset With Cast
ptr_offset_with_cast.description: Checks for usage of the `offset` pointer method with a `usize` casted to an `isize`.

pub_enum_variant_names.title: Pub Enum Variant Names
pub_enum_variant_names.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

pub_underscore_fields.title: Pub Underscore Fields
pub_underscore_fields.description: Checks whether any field of the struct is prefixed with an `_` (underscore) and also marked `pub` (public)

question_mark.title: Question Mark
question_mark.description: Checks for expressions that could be replaced by the `?` operator.

question_mark_used.title: Question Mark Used
question_mark_used.description: Checks for expressions that use the `?` operator and rejects them.

range_minus_one.title: Range Minus One
range_minus_one.description: Checks for inclusive ranges where 1 is subtracted from the upper bound, e.g., `x..=(y-1)`.

range_plus_one.title: Range Plus One
range_plus_one.description: Checks for exclusive ranges where 1 is added to the upper bound, e.g., `x..(y+1)`.

range_step_by_zero.title: Range Step By Zero
range_step_by_zero.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

range_zip_with_len.title: Range Zip With Len
range_zip_with_len.description: Checks for zipping a collection with the range of `0.._.len()`.

rc_buffer.title: Rc Buffer
rc_buffer.description: Checks for `Rc ` and `Arc ` when `T` is a mutable buffer type such as `String` or `Vec`.

rc_clone_in_vec_init.title: Rc Clone In Vec Init
rc_clone_in_vec_init.description: Checks for reference-counted pointers (`Arc`, `Rc`, `rc::Weak`, and `sync::Weak`) in `vec![elem; len]`

read_line_without_trim.title: Read Line Without Trim
read_line_without_trim.description: Looks for calls to [`Stdin::read_line`] to read a line from the standard input into a string, then later attempting to use that string for an operation that will never work for strings with a trailing newline character in it (e.g. parsing into a `i32`).

read_zero_byte_vec.title: Read Zero Byte Vec
read_zero_byte_vec.description: This lint catches reads into a zero-length `Vec`. Especially in the case of a call to `with_capacity`, this lint warns that read gets the number of bytes from the `Vec`'s length, not its capacity.

readonly_write_lock.title: Readonly Write Lock
readonly_write_lock.description: Looks for calls to `RwLock::write` where the lock is only used for reading.

recursive_format_impl.title: Recursive Format Impl
recursive_format_impl.description: Checks for format trait implementations (e.g. `Display`) with a recursive call to itself which uses `self` as a parameter. This is typically done indirectly with the `write!` macro or with `to_string()`.

redundant_allocation.title: Redundant Allocation
redundant_allocation.description: Checks for usage of redundant allocations anywhere in the code.

redundant_as_str.title: Redundant As Str
redundant_as_str.description: Checks for usage of `as_str()` on a `String` chained with a method available on the `String` itself.

redundant_async_block.title: Redundant Async Block
redundant_async_block.description: Checks for `async` block that only returns `await` on a future.

redundant_clone.title: Redundant Clone
redundant_clone.description: Checks for a redundant `clone()` (and its relatives) which clones an owned value that is going to be dropped without further use.

redundant_closure.title: Redundant Closure
redundant_closure.description: Checks for closures which just call another function where the function can be called directly. `unsafe` functions, calls where types get adjusted or where the callee is marked `#[track_caller]` are ignored.

redundant_closure_call.title: Redundant Closure Call
redundant_closure_call.description: Detects closures called in the same expression where they are defined.

redundant_closure_for_method_calls.title: Redundant Closure For Method Calls
redundant_closure_for_method_calls.description: Checks for closures which only invoke a method on the closure argument and can be replaced by referencing the method directly.

redundant_comparisons.title: Redundant Comparisons
redundant_comparisons.description: Checks for ineffective double comparisons against constants.

redundant_else.title: Redundant Else
redundant_else.description: Checks for `else` blocks that can be removed without changing semantics.

redundant_feature_names.title: Redundant Feature Names
redundant_feature_names.description: Checks for feature names with prefix `use-`, `with-` or suffix `-support`

redundant_field_names.title: Redundant Field Names
redundant_field_names.description: Checks for fields in struct literals where shorthands could be used.

redundant_guards.title: Redundant Guards
redundant_guards.description: Checks for unnecessary guards in match expressions.

redundant_iter_cloned.title: Redundant Iter Cloned
redundant_iter_cloned.description: Checks for calls to `Iterator::cloned` where the original value could be used instead.

redundant_locals.title: Redundant Locals
redundant_locals.description: Checks for redundant redefinitions of local bindings.

redundant_pattern.title: Redundant Pattern
redundant_pattern.description: Checks for patterns in the form `name @ _`.

redundant_pattern_matching.title: Redundant Pattern Matching
redundant_pattern_matching.description: Lint for redundant pattern matching over `Result`, `Option`, `std::task::Poll`, `std::net::IpAddr` or `bool`s

redundant_pub_crate.title: Redundant Pub Crate
redundant_pub_crate.description: Checks for items declared `pub(crate)` that are not crate visible because they are inside a private module.

redundant_slicing.title: Redundant Slicing
redundant_slicing.description: Checks for redundant slicing expressions which use the full range, and do not change the type.

redundant_static_lifetimes.title: Redundant Static Lifetimes
redundant_static_lifetimes.description: Checks for constants and statics with an explicit `'static` lifetime.

redundant_test_prefix.title: Redundant Test Prefix
redundant_test_prefix.description: Checks for test functions (functions annotated with `#[test]`) that are prefixed with `test_` which is redundant.

redundant_type_annotations.title: Redundant Type Annotations
redundant_type_annotations.description: Warns about needless / redundant type annotations.

ref_as_ptr.title: Ref As Ptr
ref_as_ptr.description: Checks for casts of references to pointer using `as` and suggests `std::ptr::from_ref` and `std::ptr::from_mut` instead.

ref_binding_to_reference.title: Ref Binding To Reference
ref_binding_to_reference.description: Checks for `ref` bindings which create a reference to a reference.

ref_option.title: Ref Option
ref_option.description: Warns when a function signature uses `&Option ` instead of `Option `.

ref_patterns.title: Ref Patterns
ref_patterns.description: Checks for usages of the `ref` keyword.

regex_creation_in_loops.title: Regex Creation In Loops
regex_creation_in_loops.description: Checks for regex compilation inside a loop with a literal.

regex_macro.title: Regex Macro
regex_macro.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

renamed_function_params.title: Renamed Function Params
renamed_function_params.description: Lints when the name of function parameters from trait impl is different than its default implementation.

repeat_once.title: Repeat Once
repeat_once.description: Checks for usage of `.repeat(1)` and suggest the following method for each types.

repeat_vec_with_capacity.title: Repeat Vec With Capacity
repeat_vec_with_capacity.description: Looks for patterns such as `vec![Vec::with_capacity(x); n]` or `iter::repeat(Vec::with_capacity(x))`.

replace_box.title: Replace Box
replace_box.description: Detects assignments of `Default::default()` or `Box::new(value)` to a place of type `Box `.

replace_consts.title: Replace Consts
replace_consts.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

repr_packed_without_abi.title: Repr Packed Without Abi
repr_packed_without_abi.description: Checks for items with `#[repr(packed)]`-attribute without ABI qualification

reserve_after_initialization.title: Reserve After Initialization
reserve_after_initialization.description: Informs the user about a more concise way to create a vector with a known capacity.

rest_pat_in_fully_bound_structs.title: Rest Pat In Fully Bound Structs
rest_pat_in_fully_bound_structs.description: Checks for unnecessary '..' pattern binding on struct when all fields are explicitly matched.

restriction.title: Restriction
restriction.description: The `clippy::restriction` group contains lints that will restrict you from using certain parts of the Rust language. It is not recommended to enable the whole group, but rather cherry-pick lints that are useful for your code base and your use case.

result_filter_map.title: Result Filter Map
result_filter_map.description: Checks for iterators of `Result`s using `.filter(Result::is_ok).map(Result::unwrap)` that may be replaced with a `.flatten()` call.

result_large_err.title: Result Large Err
result_large_err.description: Checks for functions that return `Result` with an unusually large `Err`-variant.

result_map_unit_fn.title: Result Map Unit Fn
result_map_unit_fn.description: Checks for usage of `result.map(f)` where f is a function or closure that returns the unit type `()`.

result_unit_err.title: Result Unit Err
result_unit_err.description: Checks for public functions that return a `Result` with an `Err` type of `()`. It suggests using a custom type that implements `std::error::Error`.

return_and_then.title: Return And Then
return_and_then.description: Detect functions that end with `Option::and_then` or `Result::and_then`, and suggest using the `?` operator instead.

return_self_not_must_use.title: Return Self Not Must Use
return_self_not_must_use.description: This lint warns when a method returning `Self` doesn't have the `#[must_use]` attribute.

reversed_empty_ranges.title: Reversed Empty Ranges
reversed_empty_ranges.description: Checks for range expressions `x..y` where both `x` and `y` are constant and `x` is greater to `y`. Also triggers if `x` is equal to `y` when they are conditions to a `for` loop.

same_functions_in_if_condition.title: Same Functions In If Condition
same_functions_in_if_condition.description: Checks for consecutive `if`s with the same function call.

same_item_push.title: Same Item Push
same_item_push.description: Checks whether a for loop is being used to push a constant value into a Vec.

same_length_and_capacity.title: Same Length And Capacity
same_length_and_capacity.description: Checks for usages of `Vec::from_raw_parts` and `String::from_raw_parts` where the same expression is used for the length and the capacity.

same_name_method.title: Same Name Method
same_name_method.description: It lints if a struct has two methods with the same name: one from a trait, another not from a trait.

search_is_some.title: Search Is Some
search_is_some.description: Checks for an iterator or string search (such as `find()`, `position()`, or `rposition()`) followed by a call to `is_some()` or `is_none()`.

seek_from_current.title: Seek From Current
seek_from_current.description: Checks if the `seek` method of the `Seek` trait is called with `SeekFrom::Current(0)`, and if it is, suggests using `stream_position` instead.

seek_to_start_instead_of_rewind.title: Seek To Start Instead Of Rewind
seek_to_start_instead_of_rewind.description: Checks for jumps to the start of a stream that implements `Seek` and uses the `seek` method providing `Start` as parameter.

self_named_constructors.title: Self Named Constructors
self_named_constructors.description: Warns when constructors have the same name as their types.

self_named_module_files.title: Self Named Module Files
self_named_module_files.description: Checks that module layout uses only `mod.rs` files.

self_only_used_in_recursion.title: Self Only Used In Recursion
self_only_used_in_recursion.description: Checks for `self` receiver that is only used in recursion with no side-effects.

semicolon_if_nothing_returned.title: Semicolon If Nothing Returned
semicolon_if_nothing_returned.description: Looks for blocks of expressions and fires if the last expression returns `()` but is not followed by a semicolon.

semicolon_inside_block.title: Semicolon Inside Block
semicolon_inside_block.description: Suggests moving the semicolon after a block to the inside of the block, after its last expression.

semicolon_outside_block.title: Semicolon Outside Block
semicolon_outside_block.description: Suggests moving the semicolon from a block's final expression outside of the block.

separated_literal_suffix.title: Separated Literal Suffix
separated_literal_suffix.description: Warns if literal suffixes are separated by an underscore. To enforce separated literal suffix style, see the `unseparated_literal_suffix` lint.

serde_api_misuse.title: Serde Api Misuse
serde_api_misuse.description: Checks for misuses of the serde API.

set_contains_or_insert.title: Set Contains Or Insert
set_contains_or_insert.description: Checks for usage of `contains` to see if a value is not present in a set like `HashSet` or `BTreeSet`, followed by an `insert`.

shadow_reuse.title: Shadow Reuse
shadow_reuse.description: Checks for bindings that shadow other bindings already in scope, while reusing the original value.

shadow_same.title: Shadow Same
shadow_same.description: Checks for bindings that shadow other bindings already in scope, while just changing reference level or mutability.

shadow_unrelated.title: Shadow Unrelated
shadow_unrelated.description: Checks for bindings that shadow other bindings already in scope, either without an initialization or with one that does not even use the original value.

short_circuit_statement.title: Short Circuit Statement
short_circuit_statement.description: Checks for the use of short circuit boolean conditions as a statement.

should_assert_eq.title: Should Assert Eq
should_assert_eq.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

should_implement_trait.title: Should Implement Trait
should_implement_trait.description: Checks for methods that should live in a trait implementation of a `std` trait (see llogiq's blog post for further information) instead of an inherent implementation.

should_panic_without_expect.title: Should Panic Without Expect
should_panic_without_expect.description: Checks for `#[should_panic]` attributes without specifying the expected panic message.

significant_drop_in_scrutinee.title: Significant Drop In Scrutinee
significant_drop_in_scrutinee.description: Checks for temporaries returned from function calls in a match scrutinee that have the `clippy::has_significant_drop` attribute.

significant_drop_tightening.title: Significant Drop Tightening
significant_drop_tightening.description: Searches for elements marked with `#[clippy::has_significant_drop]` that could be early dropped but are in fact dropped at the end of their scopes. In other words, enforces the "tightening" of their possible lifetimes.

similar_names.title: Similar Names
similar_names.description: Checks for names that are very similar and thus confusing. In particular, the lint checks for names with a single character change.

single_call_fn.title: Single Call Fn
single_call_fn.description: Checks for functions that are only used once. Does not lint tests.

single_char_add_str.title: Single Char Add Str
single_char_add_str.description: Warns when using `push_str`/`insert_str` with a single-character string literal where `push`/`insert` with a `char` would work fine.

single_char_lifetime_names.title: Single Char Lifetime Names
single_char_lifetime_names.description: Checks for lifetimes with names which are one character long.

single_char_pattern.title: Single Char Pattern
single_char_pattern.description: Checks for string methods that receive a single-character `str` as an argument, e.g., `_.split("x")`.

single_component_path_imports.title: Single Component Path Imports
single_component_path_imports.description: Checking for imports with single component use path.

single_element_loop.title: Single Element Loop
single_element_loop.description: Checks whether a for loop has a single element.

single_match.title: Single Match
single_match.description: Checks for matches with a single arm where an `if let` will usually suffice.

single_match_else.title: Single Match Else
single_match_else.description: Checks for matches with two arms where an `if let else` will usually suffice.

single_option_map.title: Single Option Map
single_option_map.description: Checks for functions with method calls to `.map(_)` on an arg of type `Option` as the outermost expression.

single_range_in_vec_init.title: Single Range In Vec Init
single_range_in_vec_init.description: Checks for `Vec` or array initializations that contain only one range.

size_of_in_element_count.title: Size Of In Element Count
size_of_in_element_count.description: Detects expressions where `size_of:: ` or `size_of_val:: ` is used as a count of elements of type `T`

size_of_ref.title: Size Of Ref
size_of_ref.description: Checks for calls to `size_of_val()` where the argument is a reference to a reference.

sliced_string_as_bytes.title: Sliced String As Bytes
sliced_string_as_bytes.description: Checks for string slices immediately followed by `as_bytes`.

stable_sort_primitive.title: Stable Sort Primitive
stable_sort_primitive.description: When sorting primitive values (integers, bools, chars, as well as arrays, slices, and tuples of such items), it is typically better to use an unstable sort than a stable sort.

std_instead_of_alloc.title: Std Instead Of Alloc
std_instead_of_alloc.description: Finds items imported through `std` when available through `alloc`.

std_instead_of_core.title: Std Instead Of Core
std_instead_of_core.description: Finds items imported through `std` when available through `core`.

str_to_string.title: Str To String
str_to_string.description: This lint checks for `.to_string()` method calls on values of type `&str`.

string_add.title: String Add
string_add.description: Checks for all instances of `x + _` where `x` is of type `String`, but only if `string_add_assign` does not match.

string_add_assign.title: String Add Assign
string_add_assign.description: Checks for string appends of the form `x = x + y` (without `let`!).

string_extend_chars.title: String Extend Chars
string_extend_chars.description: Checks for the use of `.extend(s.chars())` where s is a `&str` or `String`.

string_from_utf8_as_bytes.title: String From Utf8 As Bytes
string_from_utf8_as_bytes.description: Check if the string is transformed to byte array and casted back to string.

string_lit_as_bytes.title: String Lit As Bytes
string_lit_as_bytes.description: Checks for the `as_bytes` method called on string literals that contain only ASCII characters.

string_slice.title: String Slice
string_slice.description: Checks for slice operations on strings

string_to_string.title: String To String
string_to_string.description: Nothing. This lint has been deprecated

strlen_on_c_strings.title: Strlen On C Strings
strlen_on_c_strings.description: Checks for usage of `libc::strlen` on a `CString` or `CStr` value, and suggest calling `count_bytes()` instead.

struct_excessive_bools.title: Struct Excessive Bools
struct_excessive_bools.description: Checks for excessive use of bools in structs.

struct_field_names.title: Struct Field Names
struct_field_names.description: Detects struct fields that are prefixed or suffixed by the same characters or the name of the struct itself.

style.title: Style
style.description: The `clippy::style` group is mostly about writing idiomatic code. Because style is subjective, this lint group is the most opinionated warn-by-default group in Clippy.

suboptimal_flops.title: Suboptimal Flops
suboptimal_flops.description: Looks for floating-point expressions that can be expressed using built-in methods to improve both accuracy and performance.

suspicious.title: Suspicious
suspicious.description: The `clippy::suspicious` group is similar to the correctness lints in that it contains lints that trigger on code that is really sus and should be fixed. As opposed to correctness lints, it might be possible that the linted code is intentionally written like it is.

suspicious_arithmetic_impl.title: Suspicious Arithmetic Impl
suspicious_arithmetic_impl.description: Lints for suspicious operations in impls of arithmetic operators, e.g. subtracting elements in an Add impl.

suspicious_assignment_formatting.title: Suspicious Assignment Formatting
suspicious_assignment_formatting.description: Checks for usage of the non-existent `=*`, `=!` and `=-` operators.

suspicious_command_arg_space.title: Suspicious Command Arg Space
suspicious_command_arg_space.description: Checks for `Command::arg()` invocations that look like they should be multiple arguments instead, such as `arg("-t ext2")`.

suspicious_doc_comments.title: Suspicious Doc Comments
suspicious_doc_comments.description: Detects the use of outer doc comments (`///`, `/**`) followed by a bang (`!`): `///!`

suspicious_else_formatting.title: Suspicious Else Formatting
suspicious_else_formatting.description: Checks for formatting of `else`. It lints if the `else` is followed immediately by a newline or the `else` seems to be missing.

suspicious_map.title: Suspicious Map
suspicious_map.description: Checks for calls to `map` followed by a `count`.

suspicious_op_assign_impl.title: Suspicious Op Assign Impl
suspicious_op_assign_impl.description: Lints for suspicious operations in impls of OpAssign, e.g. subtracting elements in an AddAssign impl.

suspicious_open_options.title: Suspicious Open Options
suspicious_open_options.description: Checks for the suspicious use of `OpenOptions::create()` without an explicit `OpenOptions::truncate()`.

suspicious_operation_groupings.title: Suspicious Operation Groupings
suspicious_operation_groupings.description: Checks for unlikely usages of binary operators that are almost certainly typos and/or copy/paste errors, given the other usages of binary operators nearby.

suspicious_splitn.title: Suspicious Splitn
suspicious_splitn.description: Checks for calls to [`splitn`] (https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/primitive.str.html#method.splitn) and related functions with either zero or one splits.

suspicious_to_owned.title: Suspicious To Owned
suspicious_to_owned.description: Checks for the usage of `_.to_owned()`, on a `Cow `.

suspicious_unary_op_formatting.title: Suspicious Unary Op Formatting
suspicious_unary_op_formatting.description: Checks the formatting of a unary operator on the right hand side of a binary operator. It lints if there is no space between the binary and unary operators, but there is a space between the unary and its operand.

suspicious_xor_used_as_pow.title: Suspicious Xor Used As Pow
suspicious_xor_used_as_pow.description: Warns for a Bitwise XOR (`^`) operator being probably confused as a powering. It will not trigger if any of the numbers are not in decimal.

swap_ptr_to_ref.title: Swap Ptr To Ref
swap_ptr_to_ref.description: Checks for calls to `core::mem::swap` where either parameter is derived from a pointer

swap_with_temporary.title: Swap With Temporary
swap_with_temporary.description: Checks for usage of `std::mem::swap` with temporary values.

tabs_in_doc_comments.title: Tabs In Doc Comments
tabs_in_doc_comments.description: Checks doc comments for usage of tab characters.

temporary_assignment.title: Temporary Assignment
temporary_assignment.description: Checks for construction of a structure or tuple just to assign a value in it.

test_attr_in_doctest.title: Test Attr In Doctest
test_attr_in_doctest.description: Checks for `#[test]` in doctests unless they are marked with either `ignore`, `no_run` or `compile_fail`.

tests_outside_test_module.title: Tests Outside Test Module
tests_outside_test_module.description: Triggers when a testing function (marked with the `#[test]` attribute) isn't inside a testing module (marked with `#[cfg(test)]`).

to_string_in_format_args.title: To String In Format Args
to_string_in_format_args.description: Checks for `ToString::to_string` applied to a type that implements `Display` in a macro that does formatting.

too_long_first_doc_paragraph.title: Too Long First Doc Paragraph
too_long_first_doc_paragraph.description: Checks if the first paragraph in the documentation of items listed in the module page is too long.

too_many_arguments.title: Too Many Arguments
too_many_arguments.description: Checks for functions with too many parameters.

too_many_lines.title: Too Many Lines
too_many_lines.description: Checks for functions with a large amount of lines.

toplevel_ref_arg.title: Toplevel Ref Arg
toplevel_ref_arg.description: Checks for function arguments and let bindings denoted as `ref`.

trailing_empty_array.title: Trailing Empty Array
trailing_empty_array.description: Displays a warning when a struct with a trailing zero-sized array is declared without a `repr` attribute.

trait_duplication_in_bounds.title: Trait Duplication In Bounds
trait_duplication_in_bounds.description: Checks for cases where generics or trait objects are being used and multiple syntax specifications for trait bounds are used simultaneously.

transmute_bytes_to_str.title: Transmute Bytes To Str
transmute_bytes_to_str.description: Checks for transmutes from a `&[u8]` to a `&str`.

transmute_int_to_bool.title: Transmute Int To Bool
transmute_int_to_bool.description: Checks for transmutes from an integer to a `bool`.

transmute_int_to_non_zero.title: Transmute Int To Non Zero
transmute_int_to_non_zero.description: Checks for transmutes from `T` to `NonZero `, and suggests the `new_unchecked` method instead.

transmute_null_to_fn.title: Transmute Null To Fn
transmute_null_to_fn.description: Checks for null function pointer creation through transmute.

transmute_ptr_to_ptr.title: Transmute Ptr To Ptr
transmute_ptr_to_ptr.description: Checks for transmutes from a pointer to a pointer, or from a reference to a reference.

transmute_ptr_to_ref.title: Transmute Ptr To Ref
transmute_ptr_to_ref.description: Checks for transmutes from a pointer to a reference.

transmute_undefined_repr.title: Transmute Undefined Repr
transmute_undefined_repr.description: Checks for transmutes between types which do not have a representation defined relative to each other.

transmutes_expressible_as_ptr_casts.title: Transmutes Expressible As Ptr Casts
transmutes_expressible_as_ptr_casts.description: Checks for transmutes that could be a pointer cast.

transmuting_null.title: Transmuting Null
transmuting_null.description: Checks for transmute calls which would receive a null pointer.

trivial_regex.title: Trivial Regex
trivial_regex.description: Checks for trivial regex creation (with `Regex::new`, `RegexBuilder::new`, or `RegexSet::new`).

trivially_copy_pass_by_ref.title: Trivially Copy Pass By Ref
trivially_copy_pass_by_ref.description: Checks for functions taking arguments by reference, where the argument type is `Copy` and small enough to be more efficient to always pass by value.

tuple_array_conversions.title: Tuple Array Conversions
tuple_array_conversions.description: Checks for tuple<=>array conversions that are not done with `.into()`.

type_complexity.title: Type Complexity
type_complexity.description: Checks for types used in structs, parameters and `let` declarations above a certain complexity threshold.

type_id_on_box.title: Type Id On Box
type_id_on_box.description: Looks for calls to `.type_id()` on a `Box `.

type_repetition_in_bounds.title: Type Repetition In Bounds
type_repetition_in_bounds.description: This lint warns about unnecessary type repetitions in trait bounds

unbuffered_bytes.title: Unbuffered Bytes
unbuffered_bytes.description: Checks for calls to `Read::bytes` on types which don't implement `BufRead`.

unchecked_time_subtraction.title: Unchecked Time Subtraction
unchecked_time_subtraction.description: Lints subtraction between an `Instant` and a `Duration`, or between two `Duration` values.

unconditional_recursion.title: Unconditional Recursion
unconditional_recursion.description: Checks that there isn't an infinite recursion in trait implementations.

undocumented_unsafe_blocks.title: Undocumented Unsafe Blocks
undocumented_unsafe_blocks.description: Checks for `unsafe` blocks and impls without a `// SAFETY: ` comment explaining why the unsafe operations performed inside the block are safe.

unicode_not_nfc.title: Unicode Not Nfc
unicode_not_nfc.description: Checks for string literals that contain Unicode in a form that is not equal to its NFC-recomposition.
